html_content:
INSIGHT: The Single Most Important Thing
Ola's product quality issues are not limited to early production teething — they are a growing liability evidenced by warranty provisions that increased 5.3x in 18 months (Rs 12.85 Cr FY22 to Rs 68.16 Cr Q1 FY24). The warranty escalation signals product failure rates are accelerating as more vehicles age in the field. Together with 500+ Risks/Consumer Court Crisis cases (from 189 pre-IPO) and the Risks/CCPA_Regulatory_Intervention investigation, quality failures created the demand for service that the Risks/Service_Center_Crisis could not handle — a self-reinforcing negative cycle.

Product Quality Risk

Criticality: 🔴 HIGH

---

Current State

| Metric | FY22 | FY23 | Q1 FY24 |
|--------|------|------|---------|
| Market/EV_Warranty_Competition Provisions (₹ Cr) | 12.85 | 44.67 | 68.16 |
| Consumer Cases | — | — | 189 active (pre-IPO) |
| Aggregate Claim Amount (₹ Cr) | — | — | 4.48 |

Rising warranty provisions signal increasing product failure rates as more vehicles age in the field. By May 2026, consumer court cases had escalated to 500+ (164% increase from pre-IPO disclosure).

---

Known Incidents

Battery Concern (Feb 2022)

  • 1,441 scooters identified with suspected faulty batteries
  • 678 customers came forward for inspection
  • No batteries ultimately replaced, but incident created negative publicity

Consumer Complaints

189 active cases (pre-IPO) alleging:
  • Non-delivery of vehicle after full payment
  • Cancellation of purchase without customer consent
  • Delivery of defective vehicles
  • Vehicles failing to meet advertised performance standards
  • Improper servicing
  • Erroneous charge level displays
  • Scooter breakdowns

---

Warranty Obligations

  • Scooter: 3 years / 40,000 km
  • Battery pack: 3 years / unlimited km
  • Extended warranty (optional): Up to 5 years / 60,000 km (₹6,999 battery only; ₹8,999 comprehensive)

---

Downside Scenario

A major product recall or safety incident could:
1. Destroy brand trust permanently in a market where reputation matters
2. Trigger large warranty expenses beyond provisions (already growing 5.3x in 18 months)
3. Lead to regulatory action (ARAI certification issues)
4. Create a negative flywheel: bad reputation → lower sales → less cash for quality improvement

---

---

Data Sources

DATA: Source: PRODUCT_MIX_QUARTERLY.csv
Quality-driven demand for repairs overwhelmed the service network. Battery cost fell from $130/kWh (imported) to $85/kWh (in-house) — showing Technology/Gigafactory_Updates V-shaped improvement — but the quality perception persisted. The Opportunities/S1 X mix reached 75% of sales (ASP Rs 89,000) as consumers downgraded from premium models. Despite this, gross margin doubled to 34.3%, proving the product economics can work if quality perception improves.
DATA: Source: GIGAFACTORY_MILESTONES.csv
BOM reduction trajectory shows quality investments: 0% (Mar-2024) → 10% (Mar-2025, commercial production) → 28% (Mar-2026, NMC+LFP) → 30% (Apr-2026, LFP 46100). Target reaches 40% by Mar-2028. The Technology/Gen 2 Platform provides 68.85% component commonality across models, reducing quality variance per SKU. However, the cumulative cell production of only ~100,000 cells (Mar-2026) against 5.9 GWh capacity means the quality regime is still being established.
DATA: Source: E2W_MONTHLY_REGISTRATIONS.csv
The sales impact of quality perception: monthly registrations fell from peak 53,320 (Mar-24) to trough 7,567 (Nov-25) — an 85.8% decline over 20 consecutive months. Companies/TVS Motor grew from 25,000 to 42,000/month, Companies/Bajaj Auto from 20,000 to 35,000, Companies/Ather Energy from 18,000 to 28,000, Companies/Hero MotoCorp from 3,000 to 20,000. Market share went from 55.81% (Apr-22) to 15.95% (Mar-26).
DATA: Source: SERVICE_NETWORK_COMPARISON.csv
The quality crisis is amplified by the service network gap. With only ~300 repair-capable centers vs TVS's 1,000+ and Bajaj's 1,480+, and parts availability at 60-75% vs legacy's 95%, even minor defects become multi-week ordeals. Market/Google Play Store Rating rating of 3.2/5.0 (vs TVS 4.2, Ather 4.0) shows how quality problems are amplified through app reviews visible to every prospective buyer.
DATA: Source: financial_mda.txt
Warranty provision grew from Rs 12.85 Cr (FY22) to Rs 44.67 Cr (FY23) to Rs 68.16 Cr (Jun 2023) - a 5.3x increase. This 5.3x increase in warranty provisions signals deteriorating product quality as volume scaled, foreshadowing the service crisis that would engulf the company within 12 months.
Causal chain: Warranty provisions grew faster than sales, indicating product quality issues were emerging at scale and foreshadowing the service crisis.
Cells: Page 316: Warranty provisions details
DATA: Source: market/PRODUCT_SPECS_COMPARISON.csv
Ola's Gen 3 platform launched with 110kg weight (vs 119-126kg for competitors), 150km range at Rs 89,000 price point with 8-year battery warranty (up from 3yr on S1 X). The key weakness is still 'Unproven reliability.' Gen 3 is Ola's make-or-break product - if reliability issues persist, the company may not survive another service crisis cycle.
Causal chain: Gen 3 launch -> improved specs+longer warranty -> unproven reliability -> consumer skepticism -> must prove reliability in market -> if successful -> recovery acceleration -> if failure -> existential crisis
Cells: Row 6 Gen 3 S1: Price=89000, Range=150, Battery_kWh=3.97, Warranty_Battery_Yr=8, Key_Weakness='Unproven reliability', Weight_Kg=110
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Pre-IPO disclosure: 1,441 EV scooters manufactured between Feb 5-9, 2022 suspected to contain faulty batteries. Ola offered owners the option to bring in scooters for diagnostics and health check. 678 customers came for inspection; Oula reported no batteries needed replacement.
Context: Page 34, Risk Factor 3
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
March 26, 2022: Ola S1 Pro caught fire in Pune, Maharashtra. Show cause notice from MoRTH (May 31, 2022) stated cells used in Ola S1 Pro failed the nail penetration test conducted at Naval Science Technological Laboratory, Visakhapatnam by DRDO's Centre for Fire, Explosive and Environment Safety. Penalty of ₹1,500,000 imposed (Sep 30, 2022) and paid.
Context: Page 42, Risk Factor 15
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Ola's defense to MoRTH fire notice: (a) incident was isolated; (b) sample collection/testing not per Central Motor Vehicles Rules; (c) nail penetration test not mandatory for compliance; (d) no legal requirement for sorting cells by capacity.
Context: Page 42, Risk Factor 15
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Warranty provisions: ₹128.54M (FY22), ₹446.73M (FY23), ₹681.56M (Q2 FY24). Ola had completed NO full warranty cycle as of DRHP date since first delivery was Dec 2021. Three-year/40,000 km warranty on scooters; three-year/unlimited km on battery packs. Extended warranty: ₹6,999 (battery only) or ₹8,999 (comprehensive) for up to 5 years/60,000 km.
Context: Page 45, Risk Factor 21
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
FAME Complaints: IFCI alleged Ola mispriced ex-factory price by selling chargers and intrinsic software separately from EV scooters, to avail higher FAME subsidies. Show cause notice from IFCI (March 29, 2023) for non-compliance with FAME II phased manufacturing program eligibility criteria. Subsidy claims put on hold.
Context: Page 43, Risk Factor 17
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Refund obligation of ₹1,421.31M recorded as of June 30, 2023 for customers who bought off-board chargers separately before March 31, 2023. Ola processed refunds to >90% of eligible customers as of October 31, 2023.
Context: Page 43, Risk Factor 17
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Pre-IPO risk: Ola acknowledged that battery packs use lithium-ion cells purchased from third-party suppliers. Lithium-ion cells "have been known to occasionally release energy by venting smoke and flames." Ola stores lithium-ion cells at its facilities with inherent mishandling risk.
Context: Page 42, Risk Factor 15
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
Gen 2 battery pack improvements: 33% fewer components, improved thermal performance, IP67 rated, features wire bonding technology with lower failure rate, design features to mitigate cell-to-cell thermal propagation, advanced battery algorithms.
Context: Page 180-181, Our Business - Cell and Battery Technology
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP warranty provisions grew 5.3x in 18 months: Rs 12.85 Cr (FY22) to Rs 44.67 Cr (FY23) to Rs 68.16 Cr (Jun 30, 2023). This was the earliest quantitative signal that product quality was deteriorating as volume scaled. Each warranty claim that could not be resolved through service channels became a potential consumer court case.
Context: Page 316, Warranty provisions
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP standard warranty terms: 3 years/40,000 km on scooters and 3 years/unlimited km on battery packs. Provisions recognized when product is sold, based on management estimate of product failure rates derived from continual evaluation of tracked failure rates. Initial estimate revised annually.
Context: Page 315-316
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP extended warranty program: Optional extension up to 5 years/60,000 km at Rs 6,999 for battery only or Rs 8,999 for comprehensive coverage (battery, charger, motor, head unit) as of June 30, 2023. Back-to-back insurance cover exists for any liabilities from extended warranties sold.
Context: Page 316
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/outstanding_litigation.txt
DRHP disclosed 189 consumer dispute matters aggregating Rs 44.77M with allegations including: delivery of defective vehicles, vehicles not meeting advertised standards, improper servicing, erroneous charge level displays, and scooter breakdowns. These categories of consumer complaints in the DRHP align with the warranty provision escalation trend.
Context: Page 344, Other Matters - Consumer dispute proceedings

Connections

### Causes

- Gen 1 Platform reliability issues (first-generation product)

- Hypergrowth (0 to 53,320 units/month) compressed quality assurance cycles

- Battery supply dependency on imported cells (until Technology/Gigafactory_Updates scaled)

- Outsourced service management until Jul 2023 (Ola Fleet Technologies)

- New 4680 form factor with unproven manufacturing yield at scale

### Effects

- Market/EV_Warranty_Competition provisions explosion: Rs 12.85 Cr → Rs 68.16 Cr in 18 months

- Risks/Consumer Court Crisis liability: 189 pre-IPO cases → 500+ by May 2026

- Market share loss: 55.81% → 15.95%

- Brand Perception shift: "Exciting" → "Unreliable"

- Risks/Service_Center_Crisis overload: quality issues created demand that service could not handle

- Negative Sentiment: Google Play 3.2 rating, negative NPS, viral complaints

### Amplifiers

- Risks/CCPA_Regulatory_Intervention action: official regulatory validation of customer complaints

- Market/Google Play Store Rating visible rating: 3.2 vs competitors 4.0-4.2 deters new buyers

- Social media amplification: #OlaElectric trending, viral complaint threads

- Consumer court rulings ordering refunds + compensation create public record

- Warranty cost spiral: more quality issues → higher provisions → less cash for quality investment

### Linked Nodes

- Risks/Customer Experience Gap — Quality failures create the experience gap

- Risks/Service_Center_Crisis — Quality-driven service demand overwhelmed network

- Risks/Customer_Sentiment_Analysis — Quality complaints quantified in NPS and ratings

- Risks/Competition — Competitors with better quality captured lost share

- Risks/Revenue Decline FY25 — Quality perception directly reduced demand

- Risks/Financial Losses — Warranty expenses and lost revenue from quality issues

- Strategy/D2C Omnichannel — Platform quality improvement via Gen 2/Gen 3

- Technology/4680 Cell Tech — Battery technology quality risks
